params.name: Aulos Pipe
params.description:
On Nov 26, 1766 BC, the ancient Greeks used the Aulos pipe to worship the god of wine, praying for a good wine harvest in the coming year.
The Aulos Pipe is one of the most primitive musical instruments crafted in the ancient Greek era, and it is also a symbol of Dionysus. It is a double-pipe clarinet made of reed pipes or animal bones and an important instrument in the ceremony of Dionysus worship.
